confidence_rationale: Authoritative perspective on representation-learning -- the bottom-up extraction problem ARC-064 names as its core mechanism. Niv synthesises post-2014 work showing that the brain solves the 'what to learn about' problem via latent-cause inference and selective attention, with OFC implicated in deploying the resulting state labels. Most direct R2 review for the bottom-up extraction question that Wilson 2014 and Schuck 2016 leave partially open.
failure_signatures: An REE substrate that performs no representation-learning -- where the state taxonomy is fixed at design time and the agent only learns values within that taxonomy -- will fail to generalise across task variations the way Niv 2019's framework requires. ARC-064 is exactly the missing representation-learning component.

If REE's ARC-064 candidate substrate cannot solve simple representation-learning tasks (e.g., learning to ignore irrelevant features, learning to infer latent task states), the substrate is not implementing what Niv 2019 identifies as the core function and is misnamed.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The hardest part of learning is deciding what to learn about. The brain solves this via two complementary processes: (i) learning what to ignore (selective attention, generalisation across irrelevant features) and (ii) augmenting perceptual input with inferred latent causes that capture unobservable but task-relevant information. OFC is implicated in deploying the resulting task-state representations for downstream learning and decision-making. Representation-learning is positioned as the central computational problem the brain solves on top of value-learning.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-064 (bottom-up rule discovery) is REE's representation-learning component -- the substrate that decides what features of the trajectory record to extract as rule-states and which to ignore. Niv 2019's framing explicitly distinguishes this from the value-learning and policy-learning components, which is the architectural separation ARC-064 needs. The cluster shape: representation-learning (ARC-064) feeds state labels into the policy-learning machinery (existing E3) which handles value-driven action selection.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Niv 2019 is a Perspective-format review rather than a primary empirical paper; its claims are theoretical syntheses backed by an extensive citation network rather than a single new finding. The transfer to REE is conceptually clean but requires choosing which neural-network analog implements the 'latent cause inference' machinery. Niv discusses several candidates (Bayesian latent-cause models, attention-modulated RL, structure-learning) without committing to one; ARC-064's specific implementation choice is left open.
mapping.source_context: Nature Neuroscience Perspective by Yael Niv, summarising the post-2014 wave of representation-learning research. Builds on Wilson 2014 and Schuck 2016 (Niv-coauthored) and integrates the structure-learning literature.

confidence_components.notes: Nat Neurosci Perspective venue + Niv as the field's central voice on representation learning. Mapping fidelity high -- this paper names exactly the function ARC-064 is proposing. Transfer risk moderate because the implementation choice is left open; ARC-064's substrate design needs to commit to one of the candidate mechanisms Niv discusses.
